commentary.today_remarks: As of the 6/26 close, leadership has tilted defensive/late-cycle. Industrials (XLI), Materials (XLB), Utilities (XLU) and Staples (XLP) are Leading; megacap Tech (XLK, still +28.5% YTD) sits in Weakening with RS-momentum below 100; and Financials, Health Care and Real Estate have rolled from Leading into Weakening even as Health Care posted the best 1M flow (+10.85% excess) and Financials still shows +7.16%. The driver is regime, not just price: Warsh's June 17 hawkish hold and a dot plot that flipped toward HIKES (median ~3.8%) plus hot May PCE (4.1% headline) are punishing long-duration Tech and the consumer while a late-June AI/semi unwind accelerates the move. Equal-weight RSP beating SPY (~+9.9% vs +7.6% YTD) confirms genuine broadening into cyclicals and defensives, away from Comm Services (XLC, worst flow -6.34%) and Consumer Discretionary.
commentary.next_week: Holiday-shortened week (June 29-July 3; markets CLOSED Fri July 3 for Independence Day observed). Two hard binaries dominate: ISM Manufacturing PMI (Wed July 1, 10:00 AM ET) feeds the Industrials/Materials cyclical read, and the June jobs report lands EARLY this year on Thu July 2 (8:30 AM ET) ahead of the long weekend - the week's main risk event into a Fed with no easing bias. The rotation is CONFIRMED if Industrials/Materials/Utilities/Staples hold leadership on firm ISM and a not-too-hot payroll keeps the hawkish-Fed pressure on Tech; a strong-but-not-inflationary jobs print would extend the cyclical/defensive bid. It is INVALIDATED if XLK RS-momentum re-accelerates back above 100 (megacap-tech bounce/short-cover) or if a hot wage print spikes yields enough to break the bond-proxy defensives (XLU/XLP) and re-crowd into mega-cap quality. Watch the July 24 Section 122 tariff cliff building in the background for trade-sensitive groups.

catalysts.why: Cyclical-demand gauge for Industrials (XLI) and Materials (XLB); confirms or undercuts the steady-cyclical leadership leg of the rotation.

catalysts.event: June employment situation (jobs report) 8:30 AM ET - pulled earlier ahead of the holiday
catalysts.why: Top binary of the week into a no-easing-bias Fed; a hot wage/payroll print lifts yields and could whipsaw bond-proxy defensives (XLU/XLP) and re-rate Tech, while a cooling print extends the cyclical/defensive bid.

catalysts.event: US equity and bond markets CLOSED (Independence Day observed)
catalysts.why: Shortened week; thinner liquidity around the July 2 jobs print amplifies single-day moves and gap risk into the long weekend.

buy_ideas.rationale: Leading on the RRG with strong +6.83% 1M excess flow and a direct July 1 ISM Manufacturing catalyst; a core beneficiary of equal-weight broadening (RSP) into steady cyclicals.
buy_ideas.risk: A soft ISM or weak July 2 payroll would hit the cyclical read; FedEx's cautious 6/23 forward outlook flags freight/economy softening.

buy_ideas.rationale: Leading with +4.28% 1M excess flow, less extended than Tech, and geared to the same ISM/cyclical-broadening tailwind plus a softer dollar; copper/gold tape supportive.
buy_ideas.risk: No hard-dated catalyst - trades off commodities and the USD's reaction to the jobs print; a yield spike that lifts the dollar is the main drag.

buy_ideas.rationale: Leading and a classic late-cycle/defensive winner with +5.61% 1M excess flow; benefits as money rotates out of long-duration Tech into lower-beta yield.
buy_ideas.risk: As a bond proxy it is rate-sensitive - a hot July 2 wage print that spikes long yields would pressure it quickly.

buy_ideas.rationale: Leading defensive with +3.46% 1M excess flow; the cleanest hedge if the AI/megacap unwind and hawkish-Fed regime persist into a thin holiday week.
buy_ideas.risk: Defensive leadership unwinds fast on any risk-on Tech bounce; near-record-low consumer sentiment (49.5) is a demand caveat.

watch.note: Rolled into Weakening but posted the BEST 1M flow (+10.85% excess) and jumped +3% on 6/26 - smoothed momentum dipped after a sharp run; watch for RS-momentum to turn back up to confirm re-entry. REPL PDUFA Aug 2 / late-July adcom is a sub-sector swing factor.

Improving-early off a beaten-down base (YTD -4.69%) but flow still negative (-3.95%) and sentiment near record lows; needs flow to turn positive before it confirms - a contrarian, not-yet-actionable watch.

Rolled from Leading into Weakening but 1M flow still firm (+7.16%) and reassuring 6/24 stress-test results clear capital-return buybacks; the curve/NIM headwind caps it - watch whether momentum stabilizes above 100 or keeps decelerating.

avoid.reason: Lagging on the RRG with the worst 1M flow (-6.34% excess) and worst YTD (-9.79%); squarely in the path of the AI/megacap unwind and consumer-ad softness.

Weakening with RS-momentum below 100 despite +28.5% YTD; a hawkish higher-for-longer Fed pressures long-duration valuations and the late-June chip/AI selloff is still resolving - extended and rolling over, not a fresh-money entry.
Lagging with negative flow (-2.24%); Brent has fallen ~20% to ~$73 on the fragile Iran ceasefire/Hormuz reopening, removing the price tailwind - a geopolitical wildcard, not a trend.

risks: June jobs report (Thu July 2, pulled earlier into a thin pre-holiday week): a hot wage/payroll print spikes yields, could break the bond-proxy defensives (XLU/XLP) and force a Tech-momentum re-rate that invalidates the rotation.
Hawkish, no-guidance Warsh Fed colliding with hot inflation (May PCE 4.1%): removes the equity shock-absorber and keeps hike risk live into the July 28-29 FOMC.
AI/megacap-tech unwind still resolving after the June 23 chip rout and OpenAI IPO delay - a sharp Tech bounce/short-cover would re-crowd leadership and undercut the cyclical/defensive bid.
July 24 Section 122 tariff cliff (litigated, on appeal): a hard-dated policy binary that whipsaws trade-sensitive cyclicals (Industrials/Materials).

Fragile Iran ceasefire / Strait of Hormuz: currently a disinflationary tailwind (oil -20%), but any reversal re-ignites the exact energy-inflation loop the Fed just flagged and reshuffles sector leadership.
commentary.bottom_line: Money is rotating out of megacap Tech and the consumer into steady cyclicals (Industrials, Materials) and defensives (Utilities, Staples) under a hawkish higher-for-longer Fed - lean into the Leading-with-flow groups, but the early July 2 jobs print can flip the tape in a thin holiday week.
commentary.disclaimer: Data-driven educational analysis based on RRG/money-flow signals and verified catalysts as of the 2026-06-26 close - not personalized financial advice. Technical rotation signals can be invalidated quickly by an FOMC decision or a binary macro print (e.g., the July 2 jobs report); position and size accordingly.
